curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division (integers and decimals; checking with inverses/estimation)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Use the four operations with integers and decimals, choose efficient strategies (mental, written, calculator when appropriate), and check answers using inverse operations and estimation to make fair, sensible real-life decisions. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How do the four operations help us make and check real-life decisions?

knowing_world.focus: Build fluency with addition, subtraction, multiplication and division (including decimals), and strengthen accuracy by using inverses and estimation to check whether answers are reasonable.
key_concepts: Addition as moving right on a number line
Subtraction as moving left on a number line
activities: Quick warm-up: choose 3–5 mental calculations across the four operations (including at least one decimal).
Operations as moves: explain each operation using a number line model.
Partner check: write an inverse check for each given calculation.
Low-floor, high-ceiling practice: choose a level and create 2 questions per operation; solve and check using inverses or estimation.
tools: Canva embed: "Deal or No Deal?" app (use for comparing deals and checking answers)

human_story.focus: The four operations show up in budgeting, travel, work pay, and sharing costs. They can also be used to test fairness—spotting dishonest deals or unfair pay by checking calculations.
justice_prompts: Is everyone getting a fair share of group money?
Are “special offers” honest or a trick?
Is someone being underpaid for the work they do?
activities: Discussion: share a real situation where you needed one of the operations (shopping, cooking, travel, games).
Reflect and discuss: have you ever felt cheated because the maths wasn’t done fairly or correctly?
Mini task: write a short story/comic plan where a character needs all four operations to solve a fairness or planning problem; highlight where each operation appears.

expressive_self.focus: Make the operations memorable by expressing them as characters, metaphors, or a visual map showing connections and inverse pairs.
activities: Option A: Character design—turn +, −, ×, ÷ into four characters (personalities, relationships, inverse opposites).
Option B: Metaphor/poem—write metaphors for each operation.
Option C: Visual map—mind map/sketch showing how operations connect and undo each other.
Write 3–4 sentences explaining which operation feels most friendly, which feels spiky/difficult, and how characters/metaphors could help.

designed_world.focus: Design a practical “Fair Deal Checker” by creating multi-step real-life problems that require more than one operation, then use tools and checking strategies (inverse/estimation/shortcuts) to verify decisions.
tools: Canva embed: "Deal or No Deal?" app
activities: Choose a context: shopping, food sharing/recipes, time planning, or money/saving/splitting bills.
Create at least 3 problems in your context; each problem must require at least two different operations.
Use the app to compare at least two deals and check at least one of your written problems.
For each problem, write an inverse calculation or estimate to check your answer.
Describe any shortcuts you used (e.g., ×5 by ×10 then halve).
design_challenges: Create a set of multi-step real-life problems (at least 3) and build a simple method for checking fairness using inverses and estimation.

regulation_supports: Body check-in: notice how your shoulders, breath, jaw, or thoughts change when a calculation feels long or you feel stuck.
Box breathing: in 4, hold 4, out 4, hold 4 (repeat 3 times).
Shake & reset: gently shake hands, roll shoulders, return to the task.
Micro-break: look away and find 5 objects of a certain colour.
wellbeing_prompts: One thing that helps me with operations is…
I find it hard when calculations…
Next time I get stuck on a calculation, I will try…

pathways.name: Option A: 4 Operations Fairness Guide (Mini Guide/Zine)
pathways.focus: Create a one-page guide for another student explaining how to use the four operations to make fair, sensible decisions with money or time.
products: A one-page guide with realistic examples involving money/time/sharing/offers
Worked calculations using all four operations (across the examples)
At least one inverse check and one estimation check shown clearly
Clear explanations of operation choice and reasoning
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding: Explains meanings and connections (inverse pairs, repeated addition, sharing)
Fluency & Accuracy: Correct, clearly set-out calculations; efficient methods used
Application to Problems: Realistic situations; operation choice justified
Independence & Reflection: Short reflection and evidence of checking/correcting mistakes

pathways.name: Option B: Game + Solutions
pathways.focus: Design a small game where players must use all four operations to win, and include full worked solutions.
products: Rules and materials for a small game (card/dice/puzzle sheet)
A set of game problems that require all four operations
At least one inverse check or estimate shown in the solutions
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding: Operations and inverses are used intentionally
Fluency & Accuracy: Solutions are correct and clearly shown
Application to Problems: Problems are meaningful and fair
Independence & Reflection: Brief reflection on what worked and what you’d improve

required: Basic understanding of place value (including decimals)
Confidence with number facts and counting strategies
Willingness to check work using est
